choice: A boolean superadmin flag on the user record (distinct from household membership), seeded for your account via a deploy-time config/env value or a one-off seed script on first boot. The superadmin is the only one who can create households and provision the initial user; thereafter members self-serve invites.
rationale: Since there's no public signup, the system must solve the chicken-and-egg of 'who creates the first account.' A config-seeded superadmin is the standard, auditable answer and keeps admin orthogonal to household membership (admin need not be a member of the households they create). Avoid a separate admin auth system — it's one flag plus a guarded route group.
